title: Start Here
content_raw:
Welcome — and congratulations on making the move. Settling in the USA or Canada means dozens of unfamiliar financial decisions in the first few weeks: which bank to open, how to send money home, how to start building credit from scratch, when to file taxes for the first time.

This page is your roadmap. Pick the path that matches your situation and follow the guides in order.

🇺🇸 New to the United States? Start here

If you've just arrived in the US — on a green card, work visa, student visa, or any other status — these are the first steps that matter most.

  1. Banking for Immigrants in the USA — How the US banking system works and what you need to open your first account.
  2. Open a Bank Account Without an SSN — Yes, it's possible. Here are the banks that accept ITIN and passport.
  3. How to Get an ITIN Number — Required for taxes, sometimes for credit cards. Step-by-step IRS process.
  4. Build Your US Credit Score from Scratch — Without a SSN history, no bank will lend. Here's how to start.
  5. Best Credit Cards for Newcomers (USA) — Cards that approve newcomers and report to all 3 bureaus.
  6. File US Taxes as a Nonresident — Form 1040-NR, treaty benefits, and deadlines.

→ See all USA guides

🇨🇦 New to Canada? Start here

Whether you arrived as a permanent resident, on a study permit, or as a temporary worker, the Canadian financial system requires its own set of first moves.

  1. Best Banks for Newcomers to Canada — Compare the Big Five and newcomer programs.
  2. Open a Bank Account Without a SIN — Most banks will accept you with passport and proof of address.
  3. Build Credit in Canada from Zero — Why secured cards and credit-builder loans matter for newcomers.
  4. Rent Without Credit History — Landlord-friendly proof when you have no Canadian credit yet.
  5. True Cost of Living in Canada — Real monthly budgets by city.
  6. File Your First Canadian Tax Return — Newcomer credits you may not know exist.

→ See all Canada guides

💸 Best money transfer guides

Sending money to family back home is one of the most common — and most expensive — mistakes newcomers make. A traditional bank wire can lose you 5–8% in hidden FX margins. Here's how to do better:

  • Best Money Transfer Apps for Immigrants — Side-by-side comparison: Wise, Remitly, Xe, OFX, Western Union.
  • Wise vs Remitly vs OFX — Which one wins for your destination country and transfer size.
  • Live transfer calculator — Real exchange rates updated daily.

📈 Build credit guides

  • Build Your US Credit Score from Scratch
  • Build Credit in Canada from Zero
  • Best Credit Cards for Newcomers (USA)

🏦 Banking guides

  • Banking for Immigrants in the USA
  • Best Banks for Newcomers to Canada
  • Open a US Bank Account Without an SSN
  • Can Foreigners Open a US Bank Account?
  • Best US Banks for Foreigners
  • Best High-Yield Savings Accounts

🧾 Taxes

  • File US Taxes as a Nonresident Alien
  • How to Get an ITIN Number
  • File Your First Canadian Tax Return
  • US Bank Interest Tax for Nonresident Aliens

📊 Most popular articles

  • Best Banks for Newcomers to Canada 2026
  • Build Your US Credit Score from Scratch
  • True Cost of Living in Canada 2026
  • Cost of Living USA 2026
  • USA Budget Planner 2026

📬 Get the free newsletter

Once a month, a single email with the best new guides, fresh comparisons, and money-saving tips for newcomers in the USA and Canada. No spam, unsubscribe anytime.

→ Join 15,000+ newcomers receiving the newsletter

Need help choosing where to start?

If you're not sure which guide is most relevant for your situation, reach out at contact@moneyabroadguide.com and we'll point you in the right direction.

All content on Money Abroad Guide is educational and does not constitute financial, legal, or tax advice. Always consult a qualified professional for decisions that affect your specific situation.
